POET.headline: ROSEN, A LEADING INVESTOR RIGHTS LAW FIRM, Encourages POET Technologies Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action First Filed by the Firm – POET
POET.description: Rosen Law Firm is urging POET Technologies Inc. investors who purchased securities between April 1-27, 2026 to join a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that POET made false statements regarding its tax status as a potential passive foreign investment company (PFIC), failed to disclose negative tax implications for U.S. shareholders, and that a defendant violated a non-disclosure agreement by discussing business agreements publicly. The lead plaintiff deadline is June 29, 2026.

POET.headline: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges POET Technologies Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
POET.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed against POET Technologies Inc. alleging securities fraud. The complaint claims POET misrepresented its tax status as a potential passive foreign investment company (PFIC), failed to disclose negative tax implications for U.S. stockholders, and that an officer violated a non-disclosure agreement by discussing business agreements publicly. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased POET securities between April 1-27, 2026.

NVTS.headline: Why Navitas Semiconductor Rallied Today
NVTS.description: Navitas Semiconductor surged 24.5% following an announcement of a product partnership with Indian chipmaker Cyient Semiconductors to develop GaN-based chips for AI infrastructure and power applications. The rally was driven by validation of the company's turnaround strategy from mobile charging to AI data center semiconductors, combined with short-covering activity given the stock's 21% short interest.

INOD.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow, Nasdaq Futures Fall As Trump Rejects 'Totally Unacceptable' Iran Peace Proposal—Micron, Moderna In Focus (UPDATED)
INOD.description: U.S. stock futures fell on Monday following President Trump's rejection of Iran's peace proposal response. The S&P 500, Dow, and Nasdaq futures all declined slightly. Key movers included Moderna jumping 8.57% on positive flu-vaccine data, Micron Technologies rising 2.10% after strong weekly gains, and Innodata falling 2.82% despite Friday's surge. Geopolitical tensions and persistent inflation concerns weighed on markets as analysts warn of a divergence between record stock prices and weakening

INOD.headline: Consumer Tech News (May 4- 8): Big Tech Gain On AI Momentum, Pentagon Awards $500M AI Contract & More
INOD.description: The tech sector continues to surge on AI momentum with major developments including the Pentagon awarding a $500 million contract to Scale AI (backed by Meta), multiple chipmakers reporting strong earnings driven by AI demand, and significant AI partnerships announced. However, some companies face headwinds including workforce reductions and supply chain scrutiny.

LITE.headline: Small-Cap ETF Showdown: Schwab's SCHA vs. iShares' IJR
LITE.description: The Schwab U.S. Small-Cap ETF (SCHA) and iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF (IJR) offer different approaches to small-cap investing. SCHA provides broader diversification with 1,721 holdings and a lower 0.04% expense ratio, delivering higher 1-year returns (44% vs 37.1%), but carries higher volatility. IJR offers a more selective portfolio of 640 quality-screened holdings with lower risk and higher liquidity ($102.9B AUM), making it suitable for risk-conscious investors willing to pay a slightly hig

CRCL.headline: Is Circle Internet Group Stock Due for a Big Rally After This Positive Crypto News?
CRCL.description: Circle Internet Group's stock rallied 15.91% following positive developments around the Clarity Act, which will allow stablecoin rewards for non-passive activities. The compromise eases concerns about a complete ban on stablecoin yields. Circle's USDC stablecoin saw 28% year-over-year growth and revenue rose 20% to $694 million. However, the stock remains down 56% from its 52-week high, and analysts recommend cautious positioning given ongoing regulatory uncertainty.

CRCL.headline: My Top 2 Recent Tech IPOs to Buy for May 2026
CRCL.description: Circle and CoreWeave are highlighted as two strong tech IPOs worth buying in May 2026. Circle, which went public in June 2025, has quadrupled from its $31 IPO price and issues USD Coin stablecoin with growing reserve interest income. CoreWeave, which went public in March 2025, has nearly tripled from its $40 IPO price and operates 43 AI data centers with 250,000+ GPUs, showing massive growth potential with a $99.4 billion backlog.

IONQ.headline: Stock Market Today, May 11: IonQ Rises as SkyWater Vote Advances Semiconductor Manufacturing Deal
IONQ.description: IonQ stock surged 15.75% after SkyWater Technology shareholders approved their merger agreement. The acquisition is expected to close in Q2-Q3 2026 and will give IonQ greater control over semiconductor fabrication and packaging capabilities through SkyWater's facilities in Minnesota, Florida, and Texas. Quantum computing peers D-Wave and Rigetti also gained on broader sector enthusiasm.

IONQ.headline: What's Going On With IonQ Stock Monday?
IONQ.description: IonQ shares surged 15.25% on Monday following strong Q1 2026 earnings with 755% year-over-year revenue growth to $64.7 million and raised full-year guidance to $260-270 million. The company is advancing its acquisition of SkyWater Technology, expected to close in Q2-Q3 2026, and is supporting a sustainable urban planning initiative. Short interest increased to 23.65% of public float, while technical analysis shows strong upward momentum with key support at $52.50.

IONQ.headline: Consumer Tech News (May 4- 8): Big Tech Gain On AI Momentum, Pentagon Awards $500M AI Contract & More
IONQ.description: The tech sector continues to surge on AI momentum with major developments including the Pentagon awarding a $500 million contract to Scale AI (backed by Meta), multiple chipmakers reporting strong earnings driven by AI demand, and significant AI partnerships announced. However, some companies face headwinds including workforce reductions and supply chain scrutiny.

RKLB.headline: Stock Market Today, May 11: Markets Inch Upwards Even As Oil Prices Rise
RKLB.description: U.S. stock markets rose modestly on May 11, 2026, with tech and AI stocks driving gains despite rising oil prices and inflation concerns. The S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow all gained around 0.2%, fueled by semiconductor and AI rallies. However, surging crude oil prices near $99/barrel pressured airlines and rekindled inflation worries, while investment banks predict the Federal Reserve may delay rate cuts until 2026.

RKLB.headline: President Trump's Golden Dome Missile Shield Just Got $3.2 Billion Bigger
RKLB.description: The U.S. Space Force awarded $3.2 billion in contracts to 12 space companies to build space-based interceptors for the 'Golden Dome' missile defense system. The program aims to create a next-generation defense shield against ballistic, hypersonic, and cruise missiles. Major defense contractors including SpaceX, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, and RTX Corporation are expected to be significant winners, with Lockheed Martin highlighted as the most attractive investment option based on valuation

RKLB.headline: Rocket Lab Stock Gains Bullish Backing: Analyst Sees A SpaceX Challenger
RKLB.description: Needham raised its price target on Rocket Lab (RKLB) to $120 from $95, maintaining a Buy rating after the company beat Q1 expectations with $200.4M in revenue (63% YoY growth) and a $2.2B backlog. The analyst highlighted progress on the Neutron rocket test launch scheduled for Q4 2026 and noted Rocket Lab's vertical integration positions it to challenge SpaceX. The stock surged 13.13% to a new 52-week high.

RKLB.headline: DigitalOcean, Micron, And Rocket Lab Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (May 4-May 8): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
RKLB.description: Wall Street's momentum trade accelerated last week as earnings beats and AI-fueled optimism drove massive rallies across tech and infrastructure stocks. Ten large-cap stocks emerged as top performers, with gains ranging from 32% to 57%, driven by better-than-expected earnings, raised guidance, and major partnerships in cloud computing, semiconductors, and space technology.

SITM.headline: DigitalOcean, Micron, And Rocket Lab Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (May 4-May 8): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
SITM.description: Wall Street's momentum trade accelerated last week as earnings beats and AI-fueled optimism drove massive rallies across tech and infrastructure stocks. Ten large-cap stocks emerged as top performers, with gains ranging from 32% to 57%, driven by better-than-expected earnings, raised guidance, and major partnerships in cloud computing, semiconductors, and space technology.

MU.headline: Stock Market Today, May 11: Markets Inch Upwards Even As Oil Prices Rise
MU.description: U.S. stock markets rose modestly on May 11, 2026, with tech and AI stocks driving gains despite rising oil prices and inflation concerns. The S&P 500, Nasdaq, and Dow all gained around 0.2%, fueled by semiconductor and AI rallies. However, surging crude oil prices near $99/barrel pressured airlines and rekindled inflation worries, while investment banks predict the Federal Reserve may delay rate cuts until 2026.

MU.headline: Why Sandisk (Not Micron) Could Be the Biggest Winner Of the AI Memory Era
MU.description: Both Sandisk and Micron have benefited from the AI memory boom, with Sandisk up 560% and Micron up 180% year-to-date. However, Sandisk emerges as the more compelling investment due to its multi-year customer contracts, debt-free balance sheet, $6 billion buyback program, and lower capital intensity compared to Micron's aggressive $25+ billion capex spending. While both stocks carry cyclical risk, Sandisk's business model offers better cycle protection.

MU.headline: Industries: Mag-7 Isn’t the Only AI Game in Town
MU.description: While the Magnificent-7 tech giants have dominated the AI trade since ChatGPT's launch, investor focus is broadening to semiconductor and component suppliers benefiting from massive AI infrastructure spending. The AI-11 semiconductor stocks have outperformed the Mag-7 this year, as hyperscalers' capital expenditure drives demand across the entire AI supply chain from foundries to memory chips to storage.

MU.headline: Why Silver, Power And Chemicals Will Be The Next Micron Trade, Top AI Expert Says
MU.description: AI macro expert Jordi Visser argues the AI investment cycle is shifting from early-stage semiconductor winners like Micron to late-cycle physical bottlenecks including power generation, chemicals, and silver. He's rotating his portfolio accordingly, citing a structural mismatch between index weightings built for the software era and where economic value is actually being created in the AI buildout.

MU.headline: Why Micron Stock Keeps Going Up
MU.description: Micron Technology stock has surged 147% in 2026, driven by strong fundamentals including 196% sales growth and 771% earnings growth, coupled with high demand for memory chips and exceptional 75% gross profit margins. However, the article cautions that as a cyclical semiconductor stock, Micron's current momentum may eventually face headwinds as competitors emerge to challenge its market position, similar to what happened with Nvidia's AI chip dominance.

MU.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow, Nasdaq Futures Fall As Trump Rejects 'Totally Unacceptable' Iran Peace Proposal—Micron, Moderna In Focus (UPDATED)

Geopolitical tensions and persistent inflation concerns weighed on markets as analysts warn of a divergence between record stock prices and weakening

MU.headline: Parabolic Semiconductor Rally Is Pricing in 2028 Already
MU.description: The semiconductor sector, led by the SOXX ETF which has surged 244% from April 2025 lows, is exhibiting parabolic characteristics that historically precede sharp reversals. While AI capex fundamentals are real, the rally is increasingly driven by speculative momentum in lower-quality names rather than fundamentals, with valuations already pricing in 2026-2028 earnings. Historical precedent suggests parabolic rallies unwind rapidly and painfully, with recovery taking years or decades.

MU.headline: DigitalOcean, Micron, And Rocket Lab Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (May 4-May 8): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
MU.description: Wall Street's momentum trade accelerated last week as earnings beats and AI-fueled optimism drove massive rallies across tech and infrastructure stocks. Ten large-cap stocks emerged as top performers, with gains ranging from 32% to 57%, driven by better-than-expected earnings, raised guidance, and major partnerships in cloud computing, semiconductors, and space technology.

MU.headline: Forget Sandisk Stock at $1,500 Per Share. Buy This Sizzling Artificial Intelligence (AI) Memory ETF Instead.
MU.description: Memory and storage have become critical components in AI infrastructure, with Sandisk leading the charge in NAND flash storage. While Sandisk stock has surged over 557% in 2026 to $1,500+ per share, the article recommends the Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) as a more prudent alternative that captures the same AI memory and storage growth opportunity while offering diversification, lower costs, and reduced concentration risk.

AKAM.headline: Weekend Round-Up: Palantir Shatters Expectations, Disney Surprises And Samsung Joins The Trillion Dollar Club
AKAM.description: Major tech and entertainment companies delivered strong quarterly earnings this week. Palantir Technologies exceeded Q1 estimates with 104% U.S. revenue growth, Disney beat earnings expectations with $1.57 EPS, Arm surpassed estimates, and Samsung Electronics reached $1 trillion market cap. Additionally, Anthropic signed an $1.8 billion cloud deal with Akamai, and Baidu's AI chip arm is preparing for a Hong Kong IPO.
